behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is parser/resolver/query handoff across modules (manifest reader -> dependency graph resolver -> scanner input). A unit test of only parsing or only graph traversal can miss dropped transitive deps at module boundaries (MOCK-MISS). No real OS/network/subprocess is required if vulnerability API is stubbed (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a=no). No AI output dependency (b=no), no multi-agent chain (c/d=no). Rejected Unit because behavior text explicitly requires both direct and transitive inclusion, which is typically produced after cross-module composition, not one local function.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixture manifests/lockfiles with known transitive trees; run scanner dependency collection pipeline in-process through manifest loader + transitive resolver + vulnerability-check request builder; assert every expected direct/transitive package+version is present in outbound check payload.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at boundary between version-range extraction and query construction/response filtering across modules. A fake vulnerability API can still expose bugs where scanner sends only package name or wrong range (MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE for over-mocked internals). Rejected System: behavior text does not require real HTTP/network behavior, only semantic matching on version ranges; in-process stub endpoint is sufficient (REAL-INFRA disqualified). No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire manifest parser + range normalizer + query client against a deterministic stub vulnerability service; provide cases where same package has vulnerable and non-vulnerable ranges; assert scanner only reports CVEs intersecting manifest range and that outgoing query includes exact range constraints.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is pure ordering logic on local data structure; single-function comparator/sort behavior with no module handoff required. Integration/System/Agentic add no additional failure surface for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call sorting function with unsorted CVE fixtures covering all severities and tie cases; assert final order is severity rank descending then CVSS descending within same severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure commonly appears in parser/AST edit/serializer roundtrip interaction (format drift, accidental key reordering, unrelated entry mutation), which a single-function unit test can miss. No real OS/network/subprocess required; can run in-process with fixture content (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a=no). No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest update pipeline (parse -> mutate target dependency -> serialize) on fixtures with comments/order/extra fields where applicable; assert only target dependency version changed and all other entries are byte-identical or semantically identical per format guarantees.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires creation on the real repository, so failure boundary includes real external API/network/auth/repo state. Mocks can hide permission, branch, diff, or API contract failures (REAL-INFRA yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). Not Agentic/Workflow because no model decision quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Against a disposable real test repository, run scanner PR creation path end-to-end after a detected critical vuln; verify PR exists remotely, title/body metadata are correct, and patch contains exactly one dependency version change with no unrelated edits.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic semver comparison logic within one function/object boundary. No module interaction, infrastructure, or AI output required.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver comparator with table-driven cases (1.2.3->2.0.0 breaking, 1.2.3->1.3.0 non-breaking, prerelease/build metadata edge cases as policy defines); assert breaking-change boolean is correct.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Failure is orchestration/state handoff between semver decision, PR module invocation gate, and reporting output. Unit-testing only the semver function would miss incorrect gating/wiring. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real repo/API call; verifying that PR client is not invoked and review flag is emitted can be done with in-process module wiring. No AI dependency or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner flow with input forcing a major bump; wire real decision/orchestration modules with a spy/fake PR client and real report formatter; assert PR creation path is not called and scanner output includes explicit review-required flag/reason.
